quotes.verbatim_quote:
Pedal Point must be distributed according to the size of the unit or combo with which you
are working. In most cases, the bass plays the Pedal Point while the piano or guitar plays

the chord variations.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core orchestration rule for Pedal Point: bass carries pedal, piano/guitar plays variations.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Pedal Point may also be used at the Cadence Breaks and endings.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Expands structural locations where Pedal Point is appropriate.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Exercises 57 and 59 are not practical for playing in public. They were used to dem-
onstrate the sound and feel of Pedal Point.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Clarifies pedagogical role of prior exercises as ear-training only.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Exercises 60 and 61 illustrate the first few bars of an arrangement with the bass playing
Pedal Point, the guitar playing the chord variations and the voiceor solo instrument carry-
ing the melody of the song.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Describes the three-layer texture defining the method's practical Pedal Point arrangement model.
